channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Ugandans slam bill as mimicking Russian 'foreign agents' law
item.description: A proposed law critics say is designed to crush dissent is sparking growing outrage in Uganda. The bill seeks to criminalise anything promoting the "interests of a foreigner" and would allow anyone receiving money from abroad to be labelled a "foreign agent". In a press conference Monday, civil society organisations said the legislation replicates similars used in Russia.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UAE says oil facility hit by Iranian drone strike as attacks resume
item.description: The United Arab Emirates said Monday it came under attack by Iran for the first time since a fragile ceasefire took hold in early April, accusing Iran of sparking a fire at a key oil facility in a drone strike. The UAE Defense Ministry also said Iran had launched four cruise missiles, with three shot down and one falling into the sea.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'The highest tensions have been' as US, Iran trade fire in Hormuz
item.description: The US military said it fired on Iranian forces and sank six small boats targeting civilian ships as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, while the United Arab Emirates said it had come under attack from Iran for the first time since a fragile ceasefire took hold in early April. France 24's correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah, says tensions in the Middle East are at the highest they've been since the ceasefire began.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Stars embrace art-inspired fashion at Met Gala
item.description: Celebrities including supermodel Heidi Klum and singer Beyoncé walked the carpet at the Met Gala on Monday — many of them unrecognizable as they leaned heavily into this year’s theme, ‘Fashion is art’.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Airline's plan for fuel surcharge raises questions of legality
item.description: As jet fuel prices continue to skyrocket, some airlines, such as Spanish budget carrier Volotea, have decided to add a fuel surcharge on tickets, even on those that have already been purchased. But while the idea may help offset rising costs, some have questioned its legality.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France’s Macron sings 'La Bohème' with the Armenian President
item.description: Two presidents and a prime minister in an impromptu musical performance … It was an unusual scene that unfolded on Monday, in Yerevan, Armenia: French President Emmanuel Macron, who is concluding a state visit to the country on Tuesday, joined in the singing of La Bohème by Charles Aznavour during the state dinner held in his honour.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Vijay: From Tamil Nadu's biggest film star to Indian political leader
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, May 5: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's party is celebrating an "historic" state election win in West Bengal, while Vijay, one of the most famous Tamil film stars, just won the state election in Tamil Nadu with his upstart political party. We look at reactions from the Indian press about these two victories. Also, we discover the winners of the Pulitzer Prizes and check out the best and worst dressed attendees at the Met Gala.
item.dc:creator: Dheepthika LAURENT

item.title: WHO confirms two hantavirus cases aboard cruise ship, suspects human transmission
item.description: The World Health Organization on Tuesday said it had confirmed two hantavirus cases and suspected five others among people on a cruise ship stuck off Cape Verde. The WHO added that there may be some human-to-human transmission ​happening ‌between ⁠close contacts on board the ‌stricken cruise ship, though the risk to the wider ⁠public remained low.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Jailed French sports journalist drops Algeria appeal in hopes of presidential pardon
item.description: French journalist Christophe Gleizes has dropped an appeal against his imprisonment in Algeria in the hopes of securing his release through a presidential pardon, his family said on Tuesday. Gleizes was arrested in 2024 at the height of diplomatic tensions between France and Algeria.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'No pilgrims': Regional war hushes Iraq's worship tourism
item.description: In Iraq's holy city of Najaf, the majestic shrine of Imam Ali stands quiet, its vast courtyards no longer echoing with the multilingual whispers of pilgrims from before the Middle East war. The absence of tourists leaves nearby shopkeepers and hotel owners with little to do, their days dragging on as they hope for the crowds to return and revive their businesses.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Controversial report calls for cuts, mergers for French public broadcasters
item.description: A long-awaited report has come out in France proposing a major overhaul of public broadcasting, including merging channels and cutting costs. But the plan is highly controversial, with critics warning it could weaken public service media and do broadcasters more harm than good.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Evacuations planned as suspected hantavirus outbreak traps 150 on cruise ship
item.description: Medics were working on Monday to evacuate two people with symptoms of the deadly hantavirus after a suspected outbreak on a luxury cruise ship held off Cape Verde, which has left around 150 stuck on board the vessel with the ship refused permission to dock.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Armenia hosts historic EU summit as it charts path away from Russia
item.description: Armenia hosts its first bilateral summit with the European Union on Tuesday, a landmark diplomatic moment for the Caucasus Mountains nation that has formally declared its ambition to join the bloc and is cautiously loosening its ties with longtime ally Russia. France 24's correspondent in Yerevan Taline Oundjian has the latest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: War with Iran threatens to reignite as Trump launches 'Project Freedom'
item.description: A ceasefire between Iran and the United States was teetering Monday as the two countries traded fire over the strategic Strait of Hormuz, a day after President Donald Trump announced an operation to escort trapped vessels through the strait dubbed “Project Freedom”. France 24’s Philip Turle takes a look at the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Truce under threat as Iran, US trade fire in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The fragile truce in the Middle East was in jeopardy on Tuesday after the ​U.S. and Iran launched new attacks as they wrestled for control of the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military said on Monday it destroyed six Iranian small boats, as well as cruise missiles and drones, after President Donald Trump sent the navy to escort stranded tankers through the strait in a campaign he called "Project Freedom".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Dozens killed, scores injured in explosion at Chinese fireworks factory
item.description: A giant explosion at a fireworks factory in Liuyang, Hunan province in Central China has killed 26 people and injured 61 others, officials said Tuesday. Liuyang is a major fireworks hub, producing around 60 percent of the fireworks sold in China and 70 percent of those exported.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: French prodigy Paul Seixas to compete in Tour de France for first time
item.description: Paul Seixas has announced that he will be competing in the 2026 Tour de France. At 19, he will be the youngest rider to start the Tour since 1937.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Former model accuses French scout of grooming her for Epstein
item.description: Former French model Juliette G said Daniel Siad, a French modelling scout with close ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, had groomer her with the aim of introducing her to potential abusers. Juliette, who has asked to withhold her surname to protect her privacy, is one of two ex-models to have accused Siad of grooming.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Iran 'not even started' with Hormuz standoff as US tries to force open the strait
item.description: Iran's chief negotiator in talks with the United States, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, warned on Tuesday that his country has "not even started" in its standoff over the Strait of Hormuz. Ghalibaf, who is also the speaker in Iran's parliament, said the actions of the US and its allies had put shipping security at risk, but said their "presence will diminish". Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: US and UAE report Iran attacks as military pushes to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces opened a passage through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday after sinking Iranian boats and escorting merchant ships, as fresh attacks struck vessels and facilities in the United Arab Emirates. The escalation has raised fears over global energy supplies and risks derailing a fragile regional ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Armenia turns 'towards Europe' as Macron highlights shift from Russia
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said Armenia has chosen a path “towards Europe” during a visit to Yerevan on Monday, highlighting the country’s gradual shift away from Russia’s influence. The remarks came as European leaders gathered in Armenia for regional summits focused on security and cooperation with the EU.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Secret Service shoots armed suspect near White House after brief lockdown
item.description: US Secret Service agents shot and wounded an armed suspect near the White House on Monday, triggering a brief lockdown after the man opened fire. The incident unfolded shortly after Vice President JD Vance’s motorcade passed, with officials saying the vice president was not believed to be a target.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Can solar cars become mainstream?
item.description: The energy crisis caused by the Iran war has been a wake-up call and prompted governments to try to speed up a shift to electric cars. Could solar cars be next? Although they have been around for decades, solar-powered vehicles have largely remained experimental due to technical challenges. FRANCE 24's Yuka Royer speaks with Steve Fambro, co-CEO of Aptera Motors, a US startup that is preparing to start delivering its solar cars to customers as early as this year.
item.dc:creator: Yuka ROYER

item.title: Hantavirus: Spread by rodents, potentially fatal, with no specific cure
item.description: Hantavirus, which is being linked to deaths on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, circulates in rodents and can be deadly when transmitted to humans. Dutch cruise operator Oceanwide Expeditions confirmed on Monday it was dealing with "a serious medical situation" on board the MV Hondius, travelling from Argentina to Cape Verde. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Nicolas Locker, Professor of Virology at the Pirbright Institute in the UK.
item.dc:creator: Sharon GAFFNEY
